Overview

Current page outline

Regular Expressions RegExp ​

字数: 0 字 时长: 0 分钟

TIP

Regular expressions are powerful tools for string processing, used for pattern matching, search and replace, and form validation.

📋 Learning Outline ​

ChapterContentDifficulty
Creating RegexLiteral, Constructor⭐
Test Methodstest, exec⭐⭐
Boundary Symbols^, $, Exact Match⭐⭐
Character Classes[], Range, Negation⭐⭐
Quantifiers*, +, ?, {n},⭐⭐
Predefined Classes\d, \w, \s, etc.⭐⭐
Common ExamplesPhone, Email, ID Card⭐⭐⭐
Match Modesi, g, ig⭐⭐
Grouping(), Capture Groups⭐⭐⭐
Regex & String Methodsmatch, replace, search, split⭐⭐

🎯 Learning Goals ​

After completing this tutorial, you will be able to:

  • ✅ Create and use regular expressions for string matching
  • ✅ Master various metacharacters and quantifiers
  • ✅ Write common form validation regex (phone, email, etc.)
  • ✅ Use regex for string search and replace

🔗 Related Documentation ​

  • JavaScript Complete Guide - String manipulation methods
  • Vue Complete Guide - Form validation applications

Creating Regular Expressions ​

Method 1: Literal Notation ​

javascript
const reg = /abc/;
1

Method 2: Constructor ​

javascript
const reg = new RegExp(/abc/);
const reg = new RegExp('abc');
1
2

Test Methods ​

test Method ​

javascript
// Test if string matches the regex pattern
const reg = /abc/;
reg.test('abc');  // true
reg.test('def');  // false
1
2
3
4

exec Method ​

javascript
// Search for matches in a string
const str = 'hello';
const pattern = /o/;
pattern.exec(str);  // ['o', index: 4, ...]
1
2
3
4

Boundary Symbols ​

SymbolDescriptionExample
^Start of string/^abc/
$End of string/abc$/
^$Exact match/^abc$/
javascript
/^abc/;    // Starts with abc
/abc$/;    // Ends with abc
/^abc$/;   // Exactly matches abc
1
2
3

Character Classes ​

Square Brackets ​

javascript
/[abc]/;       // Matches any of a, b, or c
/[^abc]/;      // Matches any character except a, b, c
/[a-z]/;       // Matches any lowercase letter
/[A-Z]/;       // Matches any uppercase letter
/[0-9]/;       // Matches any digit
/[a-zA-Z0-9]/; // Matches any letter or digit
1
2
3
4
5
6

Quantifiers ​

SymbolDescriptionExample
*0 or more times/a*/
+1 or more times/a+/
?0 or 1 time/a?/
{n}Exactly n times/a{3}/
{n,}n or more times/a{3,}/
{n,m}Between n and m times/a{3,6}/
javascript
/a*/;      // a appears 0 or more times
/a+/;      // a appears 1 or more times
/a?/;      // a appears 0 or 1 time
/a{3}/;    // a appears exactly 3 times
/a{3,}/;   // a appears 3 or more times
/a{3,6}/;  // a appears between 3 to 6 times
1
2
3
4
5
6

Predefined Classes ​

SymbolDescriptionEquivalent
\dDigit[0-9]
\DNon-digit[^0-9]
\wWord character (letter, digit, underscore)[a-zA-Z0-9_]
\WNon-word character[^a-zA-Z0-9_]
\sWhitespace (space, tab, newline)
\SNon-whitespace

OR Operator ​

javascript
// | means OR
const reg = /^(aaa)|(bbb)$/;
1
2

Common Regex Examples ​

Landline Phone Number ​

javascript
const reg = /^\d{3}-\d{8}|\d{4}-\d{7}$/;
1

Mobile Phone Number (China) ​

javascript
const reg = /^1[3-9]\d{9}$/;
1

QQ Number ​

javascript
const reg = /^[1-9]\d{4,}$/;
1

Chinese Characters ​

javascript
const reg = /[\u4e00-\u9fa5]/;
1

Email ​

javascript
const reg = /^\w+([-+.]\w+)*@\w+([-.]\w+)*\.\w+([-.]\w+)*$/;
1

ID Card Number (China) ​

javascript
const reg = /^\d{15}|\d{18}$/;
1

Username (Letters, Digits, Underscore, 4-16 characters) ​

javascript
const reg = /^[a-zA-Z0-9_]{4,16}$/;
1

Password (6-20 characters, at least one letter and one digit) ​

javascript
const reg = /^(?=.*[A-Za-z])(?=.*\d)[A-Za-z\d]{6,20}$/;
1

URL ​

javascript
const reg = /^https?:\/\/.+/;
1

IPv4 Address ​

javascript
const reg = /^((25[0-5]|2[0-4]\d|[01]?\d\d?)\.){3}(25[0-5]|2[0-4]\d|[01]?\d\d?)$/;
1

Match Modes ​

Case Insensitive ​

javascript
/abc/i;  // Case insensitive
1

Global Match ​

javascript
/abc/g;  // Match all occurrences
1

Combined Usage ​

javascript
/abc/ig;  // Case insensitive + Global match
1

Grouping ​

javascript
// () creates a capture group
const reg = /{{ ([a-zA-Z]+) }}/;
const str = '{{name}}';
const result = reg.exec(str);
// result[0] = '{{name}}'
// result[1] = 'name'
1
2
3
4
5
6

Non-capturing Group ​

javascript
// (?:) creates a non-capturing group
const reg = /(?:https?:\/\/)?(www\.\w+\.com)/;
1
2

Named Capture Groups (ES2018) ​

javascript
const reg = /(?<year>\d{4})-(?<month>\d{2})-(?<day>\d{2})/;
const result = '2024-01-15'.match(reg);
// result.groups.year = '2024'
// result.groups.month = '01'
// result.groups.day = '15'
1
2
3
4
5

Brackets Summary ​

BracketsPurpose
{}Quantifier, specifies repetition count
[]Character set, matches any character inside
()Grouping, sets precedence and captures

Regex and String Methods ​

match ​

javascript
// Returns array of matches
str.match(/abc/g);
1
2

replace ​

javascript
// Replace matched content
str.replace(/abc/g, 'def');
1
2

search ​

javascript
// Returns index of match
str.search(/abc/);
1
2

split ​

javascript
// Split string by regex
str.split(/\s+/);
1
2

Practical Examples ​

Extract All Numbers ​

javascript
const str = 'Price: $100, Quantity: 5';
const numbers = str.match(/\d+/g);  // ['100', '5']
1
2

Remove Extra Whitespace ​

javascript
const str = '  hello   world  ';
const clean = str.replace(/\s+/g, ' ').trim();  // 'hello world'
1
2

Validate Hex Color ​

javascript
const reg = /^#([A-Fa-f0-9]{6}|[A-Fa-f0-9]{3})$/;
reg.test('#FF5733');  // true
reg.test('#F53');     // true
1
2
3

Match HTML Tags ​

javascript
const reg = /<([a-z][a-z0-9]*)\b[^>]*>/gi;
const html = '<div class="test">Hello</div>';
const tags = html.match(reg);  // ['<div class="test">']
1
2
3

TIP

Learning Tips

  1. Start with simple patterns and gradually build complexity
  2. Use online regex testers (regex101.com, regexr.com) to debug patterns
  3. Comment complex regex patterns for maintainability
  4. Consider using string methods for simple operations instead of regex
